description: Winning concepts log. Each entry is a concept that was selected by the design-critic and successfully rendered as a poster. The design-creator reads this file before generating new concepts so it can bias toward patterns that have worked.

entries.brief: Create a cinema poster for a fictional arthouse sci-fi film called ARRAKIS: THE SILENT SUN
entries.concept_statement: Mondo-tradition alternative movie poster. Kilian Eng Mode 1 warm palette. Dark Academia typography (classical serif with drop cap and small caps). Three celestial bodies (signature), tiny silhouette figure, dithered sky, ornamental corners, oxblood drop cap.
entries.critic_notes: Strong concept (three suns earn the title), Bringhurst-clean, Mondo-canon composition, ornamental density without clutter.
lessons: Kilian Mode 1 + Dark Academia = natural pairing; classical serif preserves Mondo mystery
Drop cap in oxblood on warm paper = immediately evocative of limited-edition prints
Horizon at two-thirds with tiny silhouette for scale is load-bearing for Kilian work

entries.brief: Poster for Tate Modern exhibition AFTER THE MACHINE about art in the age of synthetic authorship
entries.concept_statement: Appropriated Caravaggio-lit classical hand with fingertips dissolving into pixels — the 'machine' creeping into human gesture. Frosted-glass Neo-Futurism card floats over bottom third carrying minimal Saville typography. Iridescent accent mark serves as 'exhibition-open' indicator.
entries.critic_notes: Concept strength exceptional (pixel-fingertip metaphor earns the exhibition brief). Saville DNA preserved (appropriated image dominant, minimal type). Spatial-UI layer added 2026 finish without undermining classical sophistication.
lessons: Saville × Neo-Futurism works when spatial-UI layer is frosted-glass CARD, not full interface
Appropriated classical imagery with a single contemporary corruption = the strongest Saville-descended concept move
Factory catalog numbering (FAC·T·2026·047) always works in cultural-institution posters

entries.brief: Poster for fictional Blue Note Records jazz quartet album BLUE NOTHING
entries.concept_statement: Giant black vinyl record bleeds off right edge. Label uses classical Blue Note vocabulary (red center, gold ring, spindle hole). Text column left carries wordmark and ensemble list. Hand-drawn scribble = Sagmeister craft-visible signature. Concept: jazz = the silence between notes; vinyl documents that silence physically.
entries.critic_notes: Image-dominant variant of Sagmeister (second pass: v1 was type-dominant with 'NOTHING' outline letters and musical rest symbol — also strong but less classical). Image-forward variant chosen for Blue Note heritage fit.
lessons: Object-bleed template with circular object (vinyl, mirror, medallion) is natural for music/ritual subjects
Blue Note label convention (red + gold arc-text) works as both tribute and contemporary reference
Hand-drawn scribble + thin rule next to classical type = Sagmeister craft signature without overdoing it

entries.brief: Poster for fictional soulslike dark-fantasy game ASHFALL: Kingdom of Ruin (FromSoftware-adjacent)
entries.concept_statement: Contemplative soulslike frozen in time. Lone ink-brushed kneeling knight with sword planted. Huge ruined torii gate fading into dreamcore pastel fog (wrong-scale: gate enormous, knight tiny). Ash falling as ink tick-marks. Oxblood blood-splatter at sword tip. 灰 (ash) kanji red seal bottom-right.
entries.critic_notes: Shinkawa discipline preserved (60%+ negative space, monochrome figure, oxblood seal). Dreamcore bent Shinkawa's 'no color' rule only in BACKGROUND wash — figure stays pure. Wrong-scale torii creates liminal tension without breaking Shinkawa composition rules.
lessons: Shinkawa × Dreamcore works ONLY when pastel wash stays in background — figure must remain pure ink
Wrong-scale architecture (torii huge vs knight tiny) is the dreamcore move inside Shinkawa discipline
East-Asian red seal with kanji works as game-box stamp; doubles as Shinkawa authenticity marker

entries.brief: Poster for fictional AI developer conference COMPOSE 2026
entries.concept_statement: Deep-space base with 4-point aurora mesh (magenta / cyan / yellow / violet blooms heavily blurred). Point cloud (~100 dots) overlays field with glow on anchor dots. Sharp SF-Pro-esque white typography. Speakers grid below hero. Date highlighted in Factory yellow. Complete palette break from posters 1-4 (which were all warm-paper-based).
entries.critic_notes: Perfect Anadol Mode 1 Neon Data execution. Typography contrast (68pt ultra-bold COMPOSE + 32pt thin '2 0 2 6') works. Aurora + point cloud layered in correct order (blooms first blurred, points last).
lessons: Tech conference posters BREAK the paper-base register cleanly — embrace deep space + neon
Heavy Gaussian blur (stdDeviation=36) on radial gradients is the 'aurora' signature
Glow filter (Gaussian + merge) on white point-cloud dots gives them the 'data-pulse' feel

entries.brief: 25th anniversary compilation poster for fictional Kompakt Records
entries.concept_statement: Pure typography — no imagery. Giant '25' at 340pt fills canvas center. Factory-yellow horizontal bar slashes through numerals. Small 'KOMPAKT' ultra-extended wordmark above. Artist grid below. Factory-style yellow catalog stamp. Third distinct palette register (jet+bone+yellow pop).
entries.critic_notes: Most disciplined Saville execution in the set. Variable Bold trend amplifies Saville's Factory DNA rather than fighting it. 3-color palette shows restraint that makes Factory-yellow pop land harder.
lessons: Anniversary posters with a 2-digit year at 340pt work for any label / institution with heritage
Saville × Variable Bold is essentially Factory Records translated to 2026 — natural pairing
Factory yellow as single accent can be applied to any monochrome base; becomes a 'stamp of identity'

entries.brief: High-impact poster for 'Digital Loneliness' — brutalist Swiss design × cinematic cyberpunk. Lone human in darkness, face lit only by smartphone. Metaphor: screen as only 'sun', everything else void.
entries.concept_statement: Hunched lone figure illuminated solely by smartphone screen — the cold blue glow is the sole light-source in the void. Off-center framing (x=270) creates tension. Below, scale-contrast typography: CONNECTED small (64pt), ALONE large (96pt) — the word that feels bigger emotionally IS bigger physically. Brutalist research-poster annotations (SUBJECT/01, FIG. I, isolation metrics) flank the figure, reinforcing cold-studied tone.
entries.critic_notes: Hybrid Ash Thorp + Vignelli rare but natively compatible — both are discipline-first voices. Anti-design annotations add the brutalist edge without breaking Swiss discipline. Strict 3-color palette (#080808 + #4A86E8 + #F8F8F4) makes the cold blue phone glow carry all the emotional weight. PASSED layout-verifier on first iteration.
lessons: When brief says 'brutalist Swiss × cinematic cyberpunk', hybrid Thorp + Vignelli + Anti-Design annotations is the cleanest resolution — Thorp carries atmosphere, Vignelli carries grid discipline, Anti-Design carries the brutalist edge.
Scale-contrast typography (CONNECTED small, ALONE large) is the strongest way to make a two-word concept earn its meaning — visual hierarchy mirrors emotional hierarchy.
Off-center framing (x=270 instead of 306) on a 612-wide canvas creates tension without breaking composition — figure still within Swiss grid rules (safe zone 56-556).

Single-source light on a void background is the canonical 'digital isolation' visual — radial blue gradient from phone position + halation filter gives it cinematic punch without any other light sources (per brief anti-pattern).

Research-poster annotations (SUBJECT/01, FIG. I) add brutalist information-density to Thorp's minimal canvas — very specific brutalist DNA, rare but strong when the subject is study-like (social commentary, case analysis).

entries.brief: High-impact poster for 'Burn the Night' — cyberpunk street racing. Car leaving trail of glowing light burning the road. Metaphor: speed destroys time and space. Neon orange + deep purple + black. Chaotic, energetic, explosive. Minimal stretched type.
poster_catalog_anchors_used: Kilian Eng Blade Runner 2049 (illustration-posters.md) — cyberpunk atmospheric depth
Rick Owens Temple of Love Palais Galliera 2025 (2024-2026-contemporary.md) — brutalist explosive energy
Marcel WoMen's Football D&AD 2024 Black Pencil (2024-2026-contemporary.md) — editorial data-overlay aesthetic (used for trail annotations)
Mouse & Kelley Skeleton & Roses FD-26 1966 (1960s-psychedelic.md) — overlapping translucent chaos layering
entries.concept_statement: Diagonal light trail from bottom-left to upper-right bleeds across the entire hero zone, car fragmented into motion-blur mid-trail (suggested, not depicted). The trail itself IS the poster — speed has literally consumed the machine, only the fire remains. Three-color palette strictly per brief. Stretched variable-bold typography below ('BURN' + 'THE · NIGHT' at extreme horizontal scale-x 1.45/1.35) reinforces the motion. Brutalist data annotations (SPEED 247 km/h, distance vector) flank the trail — nod to Marcel's WoMen's Football data-overlay aesthetic.
entries.critic_notes: First poster built with v1.6 catalog anchors wired through concept-explorer. Four real references cited, each informing a specific layer: Kilian Eng for cyberpunk atmospheric depth, Rick Owens for monumental explosive energy, Marcel for data-overlay typography, Mouse & Kelley for translucent chaos layering. Strict 3-color palette held. Diagonal composition hits brief's 'chaotic/energetic' mood without becoming unreadable. Motion blur + RGB split + halation glow stack cleanly. PASSED layout-verifier after one false-positive fix (intentional text-layer for glow was flagged — checker updated).
lessons: Diagonal trail as hero IS the composition — the trail doesn't support the subject, the trail IS the subject. Car becomes secondary (blurred, fragmented).
Three-layer trail stack (outer soft glow + mid flank + hot core with acid gradient) creates depth and intensity without needing complex 3D rendering.

Brutalist data annotations (SPEED / DIST / Δt / vector) on a pure-image poster add editorial maximalism density without visual clutter — place in negative-space corners, Factory-yellow-adjacent tone (here #FFB347 warm orange).
Variable Bold Typography via SVG transform="scale(1.4, 1)" stretches type horizontally without custom variable fonts — extreme stretch + wide kerning = the trend's canonical move.

Poster-catalog anchors make the first render hit harder because each element has a real-world reference — Kilian Eng's Blade Runner 2049 taught the sky atmospheric, Rick Owens taught the explosive monumentality of type.

patterns_that_work: Warm-paper base + oxblood drop cap on tagline = classical cultural register (cinema, book, museum)
Deep-space + neon + white grotesk = tech / AI / electronic-music register
Monochrome + single saturated accent pop (Factory yellow, hot orange, magenta) = label / identity register
Single ink figure + pastel dreamcore background wash = games / contemplative register
Circular artifact bleeding off right edge + text column left = music / ritual register
Giant numeral or word at 300+pt centered = anniversary / milestone register
Appropriated classical image + frosted glass card overlay = cultural-institution register
patterns_to_avoid: Mixing Factory DNA (Saville) with chaos typography (Carson) — incompatible fundamentals
Hara + neon or gradients — violates his entire philosophy
Shinkawa with polychrome palette — breaks monochrome discipline
Anadol with warm paper or classical serif — anti-pattern
Josan with negative space — goes against zero-negative-space rule
More than 2 saturated accent colors in the same composition
Centered symmetric composition for Paula Scher work (she rejects symmetry)
